input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-04-29T00:58:34.036Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** â€” use `AppThemeBinding` to declare light/dark values inline:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device theme at startup **and** reacts to live changes â€” no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** â€” use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor` extension methods in code-behind:

```csharp
public MainPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();

    // For Color properties (shorthand)
    this.SetAppThemeColor(
        ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
        light: Colors.White,
        dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

    // For any BindableProperty (generic form)
    myLabel.SetAppTheme<Color>(
        Label.TextColorProperty,
        light: Colors.Black,
        dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));
}
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concept | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Responds to `Application.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`) |
| **Live updates** | Yes â€” switches automatically when user toggles system theme |
| **Override theme** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` to force a specific mode |
| **Resources approach** | For larger apps, define colors in `ResourceDictionary` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` inside `AppThemeBinding` for easier maintenance |

**Resource dictionary pattern** (scales better):

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">White</Color>      <!-- overridden below -->
</Application.Resources>
```

```xml
<!-- MainPage.xaml -->
BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light={StaticResource LightBg},
                                  Dark={StaticResource DarkBg}}"
```

This keeps all palette values centralized so a full theme change is a one-file edit.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on how to use `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI to make a ContentPage theme-aware. This is a knowledge/tutorial question, not a code modification task on an existing repo. I'll provide a concise, practical answer with XAML and C# examples.
